content.problemStatement:
You might've implemented something like - 
- Fix an index $j$ in a loop.
- For each element at index $j$, you fix another index $i$ in the prefix $A[1...j−1]$.
- Now, count the number of indices $i$ where $A[i]=A[j]^2$, meaning that the value at $A_i$ should be equal to the square of the value at $A_j$.

#### Steps:
- Initialize a Hash array: This array will store the frequency of elements in the prefix $A[1...j−1]$.
- Update the Hash array: As you iterate through the array, for each $j$, you:
    - Count how many times $A[j]^2$ has appeared in the prefix by checking its frequency in the Hash array.
    - Update the Hash array by incrementing the frequency of $A_j$.

Now, update $Hash$ according to the following pseudo-code.
```cpp
for j from 1 to N:
    Hash[A[j]] += 1
```

To count the number of beautiful pairs where current element is $j$, simply add the count of times its square has come till now.
```cpp
for j from 1 to N:
    ans += Hash[A[j] * A[j]]
    Hash[A[j]] += 1
```
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
